message:
feat(evaluator): integrate allowlist override checking into evaluation

Extends the command evaluator to consult layered allowlists before
returning a DENY decision. When a pack pattern matches but is allowlisted,
the evaluator returns ALLOW with full audit information about the override.

New types:
- AllowlistOverride: captures layer, reason, and original match info
- EvaluationResult.allowlist_override: Option<AllowlistOverride> field

API changes:
- evaluate_command() now takes &LayeredAllowlist parameter
- evaluate_command_with_legacy() also takes &LayeredAllowlist parameter
- allowed_by_allowlist() constructor for creating override results

Evaluation flow:
1. Config allow overrides (highest priority, unchanged)
2. Config block overrides (unchanged)
3. Quick rejection (unchanged)
4. Normalization (unchanged)
5. Legacy patterns (unchanged, for evaluate_command_with_legacy)
6. Pack patterns (unchanged)
7. NEW: Allowlist check if pack pattern matched with stable name
8. Return DENY or ALLOW based on allowlist lookup

All existing tests updated to pass empty allowlists, maintaining
backwards compatibility. New tests verify:
- Default allowlist helper for test isolation
- Parity tests include allowlist parameter
- Integration with evaluation decision flow

This enables project-specific, user-specific, and system-wide exceptions
for pack patterns while maintaining full audit trail of what would have
been blocked and why the exception was granted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
